qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated OpenAPI specification endpoint leaks full API structure
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a broken access control issue where /api/v1/full-spec lacks authentication, while the advisory describes an XPath unparsed-text() arbitrary file read vulnerability in html_tools.py:xpath_filter(). These are completely different vulnerabilities with different root causes, vulnerable code locations, and security impacts.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes an XPath unparsed-text() arbitrary file read vulnerability in changedetectionio/html_tools.py:xpath_filter() where user-controlled XPath expressions via include_filters can use the unparsed-text() function to read arbitrary files. The qualifying finding describes an unauthenticated API spec disclosure vulnerability on GET /api/v1/full-spec, which is unrelated to XPath processing, include_filters, or the unparsed-text() function.
evidence: Advisory vulnerable code: changedetectionio/html_tools.py:187-220, specifically xpath_filter() at line 213 using XPath3Parser without blocking dangerous functions
Advisory attack vector: User-controlled XPath expression via include_filters field using unparsed-text('file:///etc/passwd')
Advisory impact: Arbitrary file read from filesystem
Finding: Missing authentication on GET /api/v1/full-spec endpoint (changedetectionio/api/Spec.py) - unrelated to XPath/file read vulnerability
